title: How Bees Tell Each Other Where Food Is
script_summary: Explore the fascinating waggle dance of honeybees as they share food locations.

segments.voiceover: Honeybees dance to share GPS-like directions to flowers.
This is the waggle dance. But how does it work?
The angle of their dance points directly to the flower, while the duration reveals the distance.
Ethologist Karl von Frisch first decoded this in the 1920s, proving bees have a complex communication system.
Recent studies confirm that even slight changes in the waggle dance can affect foraging success.
Curious about more bee secrets? Hit that follow button!
